description: Challenging Nepali test items for evaluating TTS robustness (NepTTS-Bench). Covers edge cases that autoregressive TTS models tend to fail on, modeled after the Seed-TTS hard set and J-HARD-TTS-Eval. Categories include tongue twisters, repetitive sequences, very short utterances, long compound words, code-switched text, special punctuation, and rare/archaic vocabulary.

notes.usage: Use these items as a hard-set evaluation suite for Nepali TTS systems. Each item targets a specific failure mode common in autoregressive models (e.g., attention collapse on repetition, instability on short inputs, mispronunciation of rare words).
notes.evaluation: Compare synthesized audio against expected_behavior descriptions. Score on intelligibility, naturalness, and whether the specific challenge was handled correctly.
notes.difficulty_scale: easy = most systems handle it; medium = some systems fail; hard = most autoregressive systems fail
notes.coverage: 7 challenge categories with ~50 total items covering articulatory, length, script-mixing, punctuation, and vocabulary edge cases

tongue_twisters.text: कच्चा काँचको कचौरा कच्चा काँचको कचौरा
tongue_twisters.romanization: kacchā kā̃chko kachaurā kacchā kā̃chko kachaurā
tongue_twisters.english: A raw glass bowl, a raw glass bowl
tongue_twisters.challenge_description: Rapid alternation between /kachch/, /kā̃ch/, and /kachaur/ with similar onset consonants and varying vowel patterns
tongue_twisters.expected_behavior: Clear distinction between kacchā, kā̃chko, and kachaurā with no merging or skipping of repeated segments
tongue_twisters.description: Classic Nepali tongue twister testing velar and palatal affricate articulation

tongue_twisters.english: There is a turtle under the umbrella made of turtle skin
tongue_twisters.challenge_description: Dense cluster of aspirated affricates /chh/ in multiple positions with varying vowel contexts
tongue_twisters.expected_behavior: Each /chh/ articulated distinctly; no swallowing of aspiration or merging of छाला and छत्री
tongue_twisters.description: Well-known Nepali tongue twister focusing on the aspirated palatal affricate छ

tongue_twisters.english: For sure, for sure — say 'for sure' then!
tongue_twisters.challenge_description: Triple repetition of geminate /kk/ in pakkāpakkī triggers attention collapse in autoregressive models
tongue_twisters.expected_behavior: All three repetitions of pakkāpakkī should be clearly and separately articulated without skipping or merging
tongue_twisters.description: Repetitive tongue twister exploiting the geminate stop /kk/ across word boundaries

tongue_twisters.english: Heat the cold tea on the rice stove
tongue_twisters.challenge_description: Repeated palatal affricate /ch/ onsets in five consecutive words with different vowel follow-ups
tongue_twisters.expected_behavior: Each /ch/-initial word should be distinct; no slurring between chiso, chiyā, chāmal, chulho, chaḍhāu
tongue_twisters.description: Tongue twister testing palatal affricate onset repetition across words

tongue_twisters.english: A big tomato in the tomato basket
tongue_twisters.challenge_description: Dense retroflex stops /ṭ/ and /ṭh/ in sequence; tests retroflex articulation stability
tongue_twisters.expected_behavior: Clear retroflex stops throughout; ठूलो should have distinct aspiration from the unaspirated ट in टमाटर
tongue_twisters.description: Tongue twister focusing on retroflex consonant articulation

tongue_twisters.text: भालेकुखुरा र पालेकुखुरा भालेकुखुराले पालेकुखुरालाई खेद्यो
tongue_twisters.romanization: bhālekukhurā ra pālekukhurā bhālekukhurāle pālekukhurālāī khedyo
tongue_twisters.english: The rooster and the hen — the rooster chased the hen
tongue_twisters.challenge_description: Long compound words (भालेकुखुरा, पालेकुखुरा) repeated with case suffixes creating very long tokens
tongue_twisters.expected_behavior: Both compound words articulated fully each time; case suffixes -ले and -लाई should attach naturally
tongue_twisters.description: Tongue twister with long compound animal names and repeated similar-sounding words

tongue_twisters.english: Greens, the whole thing, tightly, Sagarmatha (Mount Everest)
tongue_twisters.challenge_description: Minimal pair chain with /sāg/, /saglo/, /sagga/, /sagarmāthā/ — tests precise vowel and geminate distinctions
tongue_twisters.expected_behavior: Each word distinct: sāg (long vowel), saglo (short vowel + lo), sagga (geminate), sagarmāthā (full compound)
tongue_twisters.description: Alliterative sequence testing sa- onset with varying codas and vowel lengths

tongue_twisters.english: Goat, of the goat, she-goat, of the she-goat, he-goat
tongue_twisters.challenge_description: Repeated /bākhr/ root with different gender/case suffixes; tests suffix articulation consistency
tongue_twisters.expected_behavior: Clear distinction between -ā, -āko, -ī, -īko, -o endings on the same root
tongue_twisters.description: Tongue twister testing gender suffix variation on the same root word

repetitive_sequences.text: राम रामले राम्रो रामको रामायण राम्ररी पढ्यो
repetitive_sequences.romanization: rām rāmle rāmro rāmko rāmāyaṇ rāmrarī paḍhyo
repetitive_sequences.english: Ram, Ram nicely read Ram's Ramayana well
repetitive_sequences.challenge_description: Six consecutive words starting with /rām/; autoregressive models tend to loop or skip tokens
repetitive_sequences.expected_behavior: Each rām- word articulated fully and distinctly; no looping, skipping, or truncation
repetitive_sequences.description: Dense /rām/ repetition sentence testing attention mechanism stability

repetitive_sequences.challenge_description: Legitimate Nepali reduplication for emphasis; TTS must produce both tokens without merging
repetitive_sequences.expected_behavior: Both बिस्तारै tokens should be fully articulated with natural emphasis on the second instance
repetitive_sequences.description: Standard Nepali adverb reduplication for intensification

repetitive_sequences.text: होइन होइन, त्यो होइन, यो पनि होइन
repetitive_sequences.romanization: hoina hoina, tyo hoina, yo pani hoina
repetitive_sequences.english: No no, that's not it, this isn't either
repetitive_sequences.challenge_description: Three occurrences of होइन with different prosodic emphasis requirements in each position
repetitive_sequences.expected_behavior: First pair emphatic, third with contrastive stress, fourth with finality; each instance clearly pronounced
repetitive_sequences.description: Repeated negation in natural conversational Nepali

repetitive_sequences.text: भन भन भन्छ तर भन्दैन, भने पनि भनेको जस्तो भन्दैन
repetitive_sequences.romanization: bhana bhana bhanchha tara bhandaina, bhane pani bhaneko jasto bhandaina
repetitive_sequences.english: Says 'say it, say it' but doesn't say; even if said, doesn't say it like it was said
repetitive_sequences.challenge_description: Multiple conjugations of भन् (bhan-) in rapid succession: भन, भन्छ, भन्दैन, भने, भनेको, भन्दैन
repetitive_sequences.expected_behavior: Each verbal form should be distinctly articulated with correct vowel and suffix; no merging of similar forms
repetitive_sequences.description: Repetitive verbal conjugation chain testing morphological precision

repetitive_sequences.english: House, in the house, domestic, every house
repetitive_sequences.challenge_description: Root /ghar/ appears four times with different suffixes and in compound form
repetitive_sequences.expected_behavior: Clear distinction between bare ghar, gharmā (locative), gharelu (adjectival), gharghar (reduplicative)
repetitive_sequences.description: Morphological derivation chain from a single root

repetitive_sequences.text: मलाई मनपर्छ, तिमीलाई मनपर्दैन, उसलाई मनपर्ला
repetitive_sequences.romanization: malāī manparchha, timīlāī manpardaina, uslāī manparlā
repetitive_sequences.english: I like it, you don't like it, maybe he/she will like it
repetitive_sequences.challenge_description: मनपर्- (manpar-) repeated three times with different TAM suffixes and different pronoun subjects
repetitive_sequences.expected_behavior: Three instances of manpar- should each carry correct suffix pronunciation: -chha, -daina, -lā
repetitive_sequences.description: Parallel sentence structure with repeated predicate and varying inflection

repetitive_sequences.text: काम काम काम, काम मात्रै काम, कामबाट काम, कामकाम गर
repetitive_sequences.romanization: kām kām kām, kām mātrai kām, kāmbāṭa kām, kāmkām gar
repetitive_sequences.english: Work work work, only work, work from work, get to work
repetitive_sequences.challenge_description: Extreme repetition of /kām/ (eight occurrences) with minimal intervening material; severe attention test
repetitive_sequences.expected_behavior: All eight instances of kām articulated; comma pauses should be observed; no looping or premature stopping
repetitive_sequences.description: Extreme repetition stress test with the word काम

repetitive_sequences.english: Elder sister, elder sister's elder sister, elder sister's elder sister
repetitive_sequences.challenge_description: Five instances of दिदी with minimal variation; tests whether model can maintain count fidelity
repetitive_sequences.expected_behavior: All five दिदी tokens and their postpositions (-को, -की) must be clearly produced
repetitive_sequences.description: Kinship term repetition with possessive postpositions

very_short_utterances.challenge_description: Single-syllable affirmative; TTS may produce silence, noise, or unnaturally elongated output
very_short_utterances.expected_behavior: Clear, natural-sounding /ho/ with falling intonation and appropriate duration (~300-500ms)

very_short_utterances.challenge_description: Two-syllable negative; must sound complete and natural despite brevity
very_short_utterances.expected_behavior: Natural falling intonation on chhaina with clear diphthong /ai/

very_short_utterances.challenge_description: Single-word question; requires rising intonation from very little material
very_short_utterances.expected_behavior: Rising question intonation on /kina/ with natural duration

very_short_utterances.challenge_description: Single polite imperative word; longer than minimal but still only one word
very_short_utterances.expected_behavior: Natural polite imperative intonation with clear syllable boundaries: ā-u-nu-hos
very_short_utterances.description: Polite imperative form, single word but multi-syllabic

very_short_utterances.challenge_description: Common single-word expression; should sound natural and complete
very_short_utterances.expected_behavior: Warm, natural dhanyavād with appropriate prosody for gratitude expression

very_short_utterances.challenge_description: Two-syllable negation; very short but must carry clear assertive intonation
very_short_utterances.description: Nepali negation particle, often used to contradict

very_short_utterances.challenge_description: Single-syllable question word; extremely short, high risk of TTS producing garbage output
very_short_utterances.expected_behavior: Clean rising-intonation /ke/ with no artifacts, appropriate duration

very_short_utterances.challenge_description: Two-word polite apology; tests whether minimal multi-word input produces natural prosody
very_short_utterances.expected_behavior: Natural apologetic tone with appropriate pause weight between māph and garnuhos

long_compound_words.challenge_description: Four-morpheme compound (विद्यालय+व्यवस्थापन+समिति) with 12 syllables; tests long-form articulation
long_compound_words.expected_behavior: All morpheme boundaries clear: vidyā-laya-vyavasthā-pana-samiti; no syllable dropping or merging
long_compound_words.description: Common administrative compound word used in Nepali education sector

long_compound_words.challenge_description: Three-morpheme compound with consonant clusters /ṣṭr/ and /pr/; 11 syllables
long_compound_words.expected_behavior: Correct pronunciation of राष्ट्रिय with /ṣṭr/ cluster, clear compound boundaries
long_compound_words.description: Formal compound used in media and government contexts

long_compound_words.challenge_description: Three-morpheme compound (संविधान+सभा+सदस्य) with 10 syllables and nasal onset
long_compound_words.expected_behavior: Clear nasal /ṃ/ in saṃvidhāna; distinct morpheme boundaries throughout
long_compound_words.description: Political compound term from Nepal's constitution-writing era

long_compound_words.challenge_description: Four-morpheme compound (जन+स्वास्थ्य+सेवा+कार्यालय) with complex cluster /sthya/ and 12 syllables
long_compound_words.expected_behavior: Correct articulation of स्वास्थ्य cluster; all four morphemes distinct and correctly stressed
long_compound_words.description: Government office compound name, very common in administrative Nepali

long_compound_words.challenge_description: Three-morpheme compound (दूर+सञ्चार+प्राधिकरण) with nasalized /ñch/ and retroflex /ṇ/ final
long_compound_words.expected_behavior: Clear /ñch/ nasal-affricate sequence; final retroflex /ṇ/ correctly produced
long_compound_words.description: Name of Nepal's telecom regulatory body (NTA)

long_compound_words.challenge_description: Four-morpheme compound (प्रधानमन्त्री+रोजगार+कार्यक्रम) with 14 syllables; one of the longest common compounds
long_compound_words.expected_behavior: Full articulation of all 14 syllables; no truncation or syllable swallowing in the middle
long_compound_words.description: Name of a major Nepali government employment program

mixed_script_code_switched.challenge_description: Latin script words (Facebook, post) embedded in Devanagari sentence; tests script boundary handling
mixed_script_code_switched.expected_behavior: Facebook and post pronounced with approximated English phonology; Nepali particles natural
mixed_script_code_switched.description: Extremely common in modern Nepali digital communication

mixed_script_code_switched.text: Meeting cancel भयो, email check गर्नुस्
mixed_script_code_switched.romanization: meeting cancel bhayo, email check garnus
mixed_script_code_switched.english: The meeting got cancelled, check your email
mixed_script_code_switched.challenge_description: Multiple English words (meeting, cancel, email, check) in a single Nepali sentence with Nepali verb forms
mixed_script_code_switched.expected_behavior: Smooth transitions between English loan words and Nepali inflected verbs; natural prosody throughout
mixed_script_code_switched.description: Common office/workplace Nepali with heavy English borrowing

mixed_script_code_switched.text: उसले 3 वटा किताब र 2 कपी किन्यो
mixed_script_code_switched.romanization: usle tīnwaṭā kitāb ra duī kapī kinyo
mixed_script_code_switched.challenge_description: Arabic numerals (3, 2) mixed with Devanagari; TTS must verbalize numbers in Nepali
mixed_script_code_switched.expected_behavior: Numbers read as Nepali: 3 → तीन (tīn), 2 → दुई (duī); natural integration with surrounding text
mixed_script_code_switched.description: Mixed numeral and Devanagari text, very common in casual writing

mixed_script_code_switched.text: Zoom call मा join हुनुहोस्, password chai 123456 हो
mixed_script_code_switched.romanization: Zoom call mā join hunuhos, password chai ek lākh teīs hazār chār sau chappanna ho
mixed_script_code_switched.english: Join the Zoom call, the password is 123456
mixed_script_code_switched.challenge_description: Multiple English tech terms plus a numeric string; tests code-switching and number reading
mixed_script_code_switched.expected_behavior: English terms (Zoom, call, join, password) with Nepali phonology; 123456 read as a digit sequence or number
mixed_script_code_switched.description: Modern workplace Nepali with tech terminology and numeric codes

mixed_script_code_switched.text: YouTube मा video upload गरेर subscribe गर्नुहोस्
mixed_script_code_switched.romanization: YouTube mā video upload garera subscribe garnuhos
mixed_script_code_switched.english: Upload a video on YouTube and subscribe
mixed_script_code_switched.challenge_description: Four consecutive English words (video upload garera subscribe) with Nepali verbal suffixes
mixed_script_code_switched.expected_behavior: English nouns with approximated Nepali phonology; Nepali verb forms (-एर, -नुहोस्) attach naturally
mixed_script_code_switched.description: Social media Nepali, very common in influencer/creator speech

mixed_script_code_switched.romanization: san dui hazār ekāsī sāl bhadau pandhra gate bihībār
mixed_script_code_switched.challenge_description: Bikram Sambat date with numerals; requires correct Nepali number reading and date format
mixed_script_code_switched.expected_behavior: 2081 read as दुई हजार एकासी; 15 as पन्ध्र; day and month names in natural Nepali
mixed_script_code_switched.description: Standard Nepali Bikram Sambat date format with Arabic numerals

mixed_script_code_switched.text: A+ blood group भएको certificate चाहिन्छ
mixed_script_code_switched.romanization: A plus blood group bhaeko certificate chāhinchha
mixed_script_code_switched.english: A certificate showing A+ blood group is needed
mixed_script_code_switched.challenge_description: Latin letter with symbol (A+), English medical terms, and Devanagari; multi-script challenge
mixed_script_code_switched.expected_behavior: A+ read as 'A plus' or 'A positive'; blood group and certificate in English phonology; Nepali verbs natural

mixed_script_code_switched.romanization: Wi-Fi ko speed pacchās Mbps chha
mixed_script_code_switched.challenge_description: Hyphenated English word (Wi-Fi), abbreviation (Mbps), numeral (50), and Devanagari mixed together
mixed_script_code_switched.expected_behavior: Wi-Fi, speed, Mbps pronounced in English phonology; 50 as पचास; को and छ in natural Nepali
mixed_script_code_switched.description: Tech-context Nepali with units and abbreviations

special_characters_punctuation.challenge_description: Em dash and quoted speech with exclamation; tests punctuation-to-prosody mapping
special_characters_punctuation.expected_behavior: Pause at em dash; quoted speech with distinct voice quality or pitch shift; exclamation with emphasis
special_characters_punctuation.description: Direct speech with em dash and quotation marks

special_characters_punctuation.text: नेपाल (आधिकारिक नाम: सङ्घीय लोकतान्त्रिक गणतन्त्र नेपाल) दक्षिण एसियाको देश हो।
special_characters_punctuation.romanization: nepāl (ādhikārik nām: saṅghīya loktāntrik gaṇtantra nepāl) dakṣiṇ esiyāko desh ho.
special_characters_punctuation.english: Nepal (official name: Federal Democratic Republic of Nepal) is a country in South Asia.
special_characters_punctuation.challenge_description: Parenthetical aside with colon; requires natural prosodic bracketing of the parenthetical content
special_characters_punctuation.expected_behavior: Parenthetical content read with lowered pitch and faster pace; main clause resumes naturally after closing paren

special_characters_punctuation.english: What... what was I saying... I forgot!
special_characters_punctuation.challenge_description: Ellipses indicating hesitation pauses followed by exclamation; tests hesitation prosody
special_characters_punctuation.expected_behavior: Natural hesitation pauses at ellipses; final exclamation with surprise/frustration emphasis
special_characters_punctuation.description: Conversational Nepali with hesitation markers and exclamation

special_characters_punctuation.english: "Are you coming or not?" — she asked.
special_characters_punctuation.challenge_description: Quoted question with alternative question (कि) inside quotes, followed by attribution with em dash
special_characters_punctuation.expected_behavior: Rising intonation on the quoted question; natural pause at em dash; attribution in neutral narrative tone
special_characters_punctuation.description: Quoted question with alternative construction and speech attribution

special_characters_punctuation.text: उसको उमेर ४५–५० वर्ष जति हो; ठ्याक्कै थाहा छैन।
special_characters_punctuation.romanization: usko umer 45–50 barṣa jati ho; ṭhyākkay thāhā chhaina.
special_characters_punctuation.english: His age is about 45–50 years; I don't know exactly.
special_characters_punctuation.challenge_description: En dash for number range, Devanagari numerals, and semicolon; tests range reading and clause boundary
special_characters_punctuation.expected_behavior: 45–50 read as 'पैँतालीसदेखि पचास' with natural range intonation; semicolon produces clause boundary pause
special_characters_punctuation.description: Sentence with numeric range, semicolon clause boundary, and Devanagari numerals

special_characters_punctuation.text: नेपाली भाषा — जसलाई 'खस कुरा' पनि भनिन्छ — दक्षिण एसियाली भाषा हो।
special_characters_punctuation.romanization: nepālī bhāṣā — jaslāī 'khas kurā' pani bhaninchha — dakṣiṇ esiyālī bhāṣā ho.
special_characters_punctuation.english: Nepali language — also called 'Khas Kura' — is a South Asian language.
special_characters_punctuation.challenge_description: Double em dash parenthetical with single quotes inside; complex prosodic structure
special_characters_punctuation.expected_behavior: Em dash parenthetical read with lowered pitch; single-quoted term slightly emphasized; smooth return to main clause
special_characters_punctuation.description: Academic-style sentence with em dash parenthetical and embedded quotes

rare_archaic_words.text: अभिलेखागारमा पुरातात्त्विक दस्तावेजहरू सुरक्षित राखिएका छन्।
rare_archaic_words.romanization: abhilekhāgārmā purātāttwik dastāvejharū surakṣit rākhiekā chhan.
rare_archaic_words.english: Archaeological documents are kept safe in the archive.
rare_archaic_words.challenge_description: Rare Sanskrit-origin words अभिलेखागार and पुरातात्त्विक with complex consonant clusters
rare_archaic_words.expected_behavior: Clear articulation of अभिलेखागार (abhi-lekhā-gār) and पुरातात्त्विक (purā-tāttvik) with correct clusters
rare_archaic_words.description: Formal/literary Nepali using Sanskrit-origin archival terminology

rare_archaic_words.text: सर्वोच्च न्यायालयको न्यायाधीशले यथास्थितिमा रिट जारी गर्नुभयो।
rare_archaic_words.romanization: sarbochcha nyāyālayko nyāyādhīshle yathāsthitimā riṭ jārī garnubhayo.
rare_archaic_words.english: The Supreme Court justice issued a writ in the status quo.
rare_archaic_words.challenge_description: Legal Nepali with Sanskrit compounds सर्वोच्च, न्यायाधीश, यथास्थिति; rare in casual speech
rare_archaic_words.expected_behavior: Correct pronunciation of legal terms; सर्वोच्च with geminate /chch/; न्यायाधीश with /nyāyā-dhīsh/
rare_archaic_words.description: Formal legal Nepali from Supreme Court context

rare_archaic_words.english: The archaeological study of the royal palace was completed.
rare_archaic_words.challenge_description: पुरातत्त्वशास्त्रीय is an extremely long Sanskrit compound with /ttv/ and /shāstr/ clusters
rare_archaic_words.expected_behavior: Full articulation of purā-tattva-shāstrīya without simplifying the consonant clusters

rare_archaic_words.text: अन्तर्राष्ट्रिय मानवाधिकार प्रतिष्ठानको प्रतिवेदन प्रकाशित भएको छ।
rare_archaic_words.romanization: antarrāṣṭriya mānavādhikār pratiṣṭhānko pratibedan prakāshit bhaeko chha.
rare_archaic_words.english: The report of the international human rights institution has been published.
rare_archaic_words.challenge_description: Dense formal vocabulary: अन्तर्राष्ट्रिय, मानवाधिकार, प्रतिष्ठान, प्रतिवेदन — all Sanskrit compounds
rare_archaic_words.expected_behavior: Each Sanskrit compound correctly segmented and pronounced; /ṣṭr/ cluster in राष्ट्रिय and /ṣṭh/ in प्रतिष्ठान clear
rare_archaic_words.description: Formal institutional Nepali from human rights / NGO context

rare_archaic_words.text: वेदान्तदर्शनको ज्ञानमीमांसा अत्यन्त गहन विषय हो।
rare_archaic_words.romanization: vedāntadarshanko jñānamīmāṃsā atyanta gahana biṣaya ho.
rare_archaic_words.english: The epistemology of Vedanta philosophy is a very profound subject.
rare_archaic_words.challenge_description: Philosophical Sanskrit terms: वेदान्तदर्शन, ज्ञानमीमांसा — extremely rare in spoken Nepali
rare_archaic_words.expected_behavior: Correct pronunciation of ज्ञ as /jña/ (not /gya/); मीमांसा with nasalized vowel; all clusters intact
rare_archaic_words.description: Philosophical/academic Nepali using classical Sanskrit terminology

rare_archaic_words.text: लोकसेवा आयोगको परीक्षामा निबन्धात्मक प्रश्न सोधिन्छ।
rare_archaic_words.romanization: lokasevā āyogko parīkṣāmā nibandhātmak prashna sodhinchha.
rare_archaic_words.english: Essay-type questions are asked in the Public Service Commission exam.
rare_archaic_words.challenge_description: Formal administrative terms: लोकसेवा आयोग, निबन्धात्मक — common in government but rare in casual speech
rare_archaic_words.expected_behavior: निबन्धात्मक correctly produced with /nibandh-ātmak/ boundary; formal register prosody
rare_archaic_words.description: Government examination Nepali with formal administrative vocabulary
